proof_boundary: Ordinary positive spectral moment obstruction only; not a proof against nonordinary positive transforms, positive kernels, non-power bases, signed-to-positive conjugate coefficient extractions, Xi/Phi-specific identities, Schur positivity, RH, and not Lambda <= 0.

objects.positive_measure_requirement: If mu_m are ordinary moments of a positive measure, every finite Hankel matrix (mu_{i+j}) is positive semidefinite, so Delta_r>=0 for every r.

symbolic_rows.statement: For H(t)=1+g_1*t+g_2*t^2+..., E(t)=1/H(-t) has mu_0=1, mu_1=g_1, and mu_2=g_1^2-g_2, hence Delta_2=mu_0*mu_2-mu_1^2=-g_2.
symbolic_rows.consequence: For any window with g_2>0, the ordinary moment Hankel matrix [[mu_0,mu_1],[mu_1,mu_2]] is not positive semidefinite.
symbolic_rows.proof_boundary: Exact algebraic obstruction to ordinary positive moment representation only.

symbolic_rows.statement: The signed J-fraction diagnostic records (-1)^(r(r-1)/2) Delta_r>0 on the finite grid; at r=2 this means Delta_2<0.
symbolic_rows.consequence: The finite signed-Hankel signature is structurally incompatible with a positive measure whose ordinary power moments are mu_m.
symbolic_rows.proof_boundary: Finite-grid diagnostic conflict only; not an all-order theorem.

symbolic_rows.statement: A positive spectral transform can survive only if it is not the ordinary moment representation mu_m=int x^m dnu(x) of a positive measure.
symbolic_rows.consequence: A surviving transform must use a non-power basis, a positive kernel, a different coefficient extraction, or an Xi/Phi-specific positive functional whose Taylor coefficients are nevertheless exactly mu_m.
symbolic_rows.proof_boundary: Route separation only; no surviving transform is constructed here.

route_consequences: Reject a positive spectral-measure proof if it claims mu_m are ordinary power moments of a positive measure.
Keep pr_01 live only for nonordinary positive transforms whose coefficient extraction is exactly E(t) without requiring Hankel PSD for the raw mu_m sequence.
Keep pr_02 live for Xi/Phi-specific positive kernel identities whose Taylor coefficients are mu_m but whose positivity is not an ordinary moment-Hankel claim.

summary.main_finding: The ordinary positive spectral moment interpretation of pr_01 is impossible: Delta_2=-g_2 symbolically, and the finite signed-Hankel scout validates 735 checked Delta_2<0 rows. A nonordinary positive spectral transform can remain live only if it is not an ordinary power-moment representation of the raw reciprocal coefficients mu_m.

invariants: The artifact rejects only ordinary positive moment representations mu_m=int x^m dnu(x).
The artifact does not reject nonordinary positive transforms, positive kernels, or Xi/Phi-specific readouts.
No row assumes endpoint PF, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
The artifact does not prove reciprocal coefficient positivity.
